am_articles.title: Markets News, June 3, 2026: Indexes Fall, Ending Run of Records; S&P 500 Snaps 9-Session Winning Streak; Bond Yields, Oil Prices Gain
am_articles.description: S&P 500 -0.74% (7,553.68), Dow -1.2% (>-600 pts), Nasdaq -0.9%. IT and Consumer Discretionary -1.5% each. Energy +1.9% on oil surge. IBM, CRM, MSFT, NVDA fell 3-7%. LITE -9% worst performer. 10Y yield >4.49%. WTI +2.3% near $96/bbl. DXY 99.52. Bitcoin ~$65,600.

am_articles.title: S&P 500, Dow Jones, Nasdaq Index Push to Fresh Highs, While Russell 2000 Lags
am_articles.description: Nasdaq, S&P 500, and Dow hit record closes June 1-2 while Russell 2000 declined. Tech/AI leadership concentrated. Consumer Discretionary under pressure (AMZN, COST below 200-day MA). Oil surged +5.91% to $92.52 on Iran tensions. Growing divergence beneath surface.

am_articles.title: Global Economy Briefing — June 2, 2026: Records, Oil Spike, Manufacturing PMI at 4-Year High
am_articles.description: S&P 500 7,599.96, Nasdaq 27,086.81, Dow 51,078.88 all records. VIX 16.05 (+4.77%). WTI +5.93% $92.54, Brent $97.79. 10Y 4.51% (+6bps). ISM Mfg PMI 54.0 (4yr high). Atlanta Fed GDPNow Q2 cut to 3.0%. Brazil Mfg PMI crashed to 49.1. Fed hike odds ~60% by Dec.

am_articles.title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates at 3.75% — Deepest Dissent Since 1992 (8-4 Vote). Next FOMC June 17
am_articles.description: Fed funds rate 3.50-3.75% held for 3rd straight meeting. April vote 8-4 with 4 dissents (most since Oct 1992). CPI 3.8% YoY, unemployment 4.3%. FOMC minutes: majority sees potential rate HIKES if inflation persists above 2%. Next decision June 17, consensus: no change.

am_articles.title: Treasury Yields June 4: 4W Bill 3.69%, 2Y 4.06%, 10Y 4.48%, 30Y 4.98% — Curve Steady
am_articles.description: US 4-week bill 3.69% (+1bp). 2Y 4.06% (-2.2bp). 10Y 4.48% (-2.1bp). 30Y 4.98% (-1.1bp). 2s10s spread ~42bp. Short-end down 56bp YoY. Market pricing no rate changes through 2026 per Trading Economics models.

am_articles.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Q2 FY2026 Earnings: Revenue $22.2B (+48% YoY), EPS $2.44 Beat, AI Revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY), Q3 Guide $29.4B
am_articles.description: AVGO delivered record quarter. AI semiconductor revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY). Q3 guidance ~$29.4B revenue, AI >$16B (>200% YoY). Adj EBITDA margin 69%. FCF $10.3B. Stock at $479.23 (-0.5% pre-earnings), +40% YTD. Google $80B AI buildout deal, Meta partnership.

am_articles.title: CrowdStrike (CRWD) Q1 FY2027: Revenue $1.39B (+26% YoY), EPS $1.10 Beat, But Stock -10.3% After-Hours on 'Mythos AI' Narrative
am_articles.description: CRWD beat on revenue ($1.39B vs $1.36B) and EPS ($1.10 vs $1.07). ARR $5.51B (+24% YoY). Raised FY2027 net new ARR guide +$50M. FCF record $468.5M (34% margin). CEO Kurtz: 'Mythos AI inflection point' — only cyber firm selected by both Anthropic and OpenAI. Stock -2.78% regular, -10.3% after-hours to $670.80. High expectations already priced in.

am_articles.title: Oil Prices Surge as Iran Suspends US Talks, Strait of Hormuz Supply Fears Return — Brent $94.98, WTI $92.16
am_articles.description: Brent +4.2% to $94.98, WTI +5.5% to $92.16. Iran's Tasnim reported Tehran halted talks after renewed Lebanon clashes. Strait of Hormuz (20% of global oil/LNG) remains disrupted. Goldman: crude may stay $90 even if strait reopens. OECD: Iran conflict 'dominant force' on global economy. WTI currently $95.31 (-0.74% today June 4).

am_articles.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) $214.75 (-3.62% June 3) — New PC Chip, DGX Station, Cosmos 3, Robotaxi Platform Announced
am_articles.description: NVDA -3.62% to $214.75 (high $222.82, low $214.51). June 1: +6.26% on PC chip launch. New products: DGX Station for Windows (1T parameter, 748GB memory), RTX Spark (1 petaflop AI superchip), Cosmos 3 world model, Alpamayo 2 Super VLA model for robotaxis, Isaac GR00T humanoid robot. TSMC adopting NVIDIA AI for fabs. $1.5B Healthy Taiwan AI initiative. 52-wk high $236.54 (May 14), low $138.83.

am_articles.title: Space Stocks Fall: ASTS -5%, RKLB -3% After Blue Origin Hotfire Explosion — Analyst Pushes Coverage to 2028
am_articles.description: Blue Origin New Glenn 'anomaly' during hotfire test at Cape Canaveral. Analyst Tim Farrar: only 3-5 F9 launches realistic in 2026, continuous AST service not before 2028. Deutsche Bank downgraded ASTS to Hold. ASTS retail sentiment 'extremely bullish' despite +381% 1yr return. RKLB -3%, LUNR -3%, RDW -4%. ASTS CEO maintains 'on target' for 45 satellites.

am_articles.title: SpaceX IPO at $135/Share, 555.6M Shares, Raising ~$75B — Largest IPO Ever, $1.75T Valuation
am_articles.description: SpaceX IPO filing: $135/share, 555.6M shares → ~$75B raise, $1.75T implied valuation. Would place among S&P 500's top members by market cap. Could launch as soon as next week. Part of expected wave of mega-listings including Anthropic, OpenAI. SpaceX identified ASTS as direct competitor in direct-to-device communications. TAM estimated at $28.5T.

am_articles.title: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) Q3 FY2026: EPS $0.85 Beat, Revenue $3.0B, Stock -5.7% on Lofty Expectations
am_articles.description: PANW beat fiscal Q3: adj EPS $0.85 on $3.0B revenue. Dan Ives (Wedbush) called it 'Wembanyama-Like Quarter.' Stock fell 5.7% despite beat — high expectations after record highs. Cyber sector momentum remains with AI tailwinds.

am_articles.title: Trump Proposes 10-12.5% Tariffs on 60 Countries Over Forced-Labor; Cuts Industrial Equipment Tariffs to 15%
am_articles.description: New proposal: 10-12.5% tariffs on 60 countries (Canada, Mexico, EU, China) over forced-labor safeguards. 76-page exemption list. Separately cut tariffs on combines, bulldozers, forklifts to 15% (10% if mostly US metal). Hearing July 7. Context: Supreme Court struck down most emergency-declaration tariffs in 2025.

am_articles.title: US Gas Prices $4.56/Gallon, Up >40% Since February — Consumer Behavior Shifting
am_articles.description: National avg $4.56/gal, >40% since late Feb when Iran closed Strait of Hormuz. Consumers making smaller, more frequent fill-ups. Walmart CFO: gallons per fill-up fell below 10 for first time since 2022. Costco/Sam's Club/BJ's seeing surge in visits. OECD: conflict 'dominant force' shaping global economy.

am_articles.title: MicroStrategy (MSTR) Sells 32 BTC for First Time Since 2022 — Stock Drops 4.72%
am_articles.description: MSTR sold 32 BTC (first sale since Dec 2022 tax-loss harvesting). June 1 8-K filing revealed sale. Stock dropped $7.52 (-4.72%) to $151.57. Bitcoin slid toward $71,400 (-2.77%). MSTR holds ~843,706 BTC at avg cost ~$65,000. Total holdings valued at ~$55B at current BTC prices.

USD supported by Fed hawkishness, Mideast geopolitical risk premium. Range-bound outlook for June per MTFX. JPY weakness persists despite potential BoJ normalization.

am_articles.description: LITE -9% on June 3, worst S&P 500/ Nasdaq performer. Coherent (COHR) -2.5%. Both up >125-150% YTD. Profit-taking after massive runs in photonics/AI infrastructure names. LITE short float 11.77%, high vol name. Sector rotation out of extended tech.

am_articles.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI (May) Hits 4-Year High at 54.0, New Orders 56.8 — But GDPNow Q2 Cut to 3.0%

am_articles.title: Credit Markets: IG Yields Near 5%, HY Cautious — Schwab 'Up-in-Quality' Theme Maintained
am_articles.description: IG corporate bond yields near 5% — highest carry in over a decade. Credit spreads remain tight. Schwab maintains 'up-in-quality' theme: IG attractive, HY cautious on rich valuations. IG yield curve positively sloped rewarding duration. Corporate credit quality improving. Bank loan market also watched.

US-Iran talks status critical; formal collapse could push oil through $95 and 10Y above 4.60%. Fed hike odds ~60% by December.
